apiJob.title: Motion Designer
apiJob.description: Our StoryPeopleHow we hireTeamsEngineering teamDesign teamProduct teamCustomer experience teamLocationsLife at MiroOpen positionsMenuOur StoryPeopleHow we hireTeamsEngineering teamDesign teamProduct teamCustomer experience teamLocationsLife at MiroOpen positionsHomeOpen positionsVacancyMotion DesignerDepartment: MarketingLocations: London, New YorkJob Requisition ID: JR001588Apply nowAbout the Team The Brand team at Miro is responsible for how the company shows up in the world: from campaigns and product launches to events and social channels. We're a small, collaborative group of creatives who work across the full spectrum of brand expression, partnering closely with product marketing, corporate marketing, and events. We move fast, care deeply about craft, and are increasingly leaning into AI tools to push what's possible. About the Role We're looking for an ambitious, well-rounded Motion Designer to join our Brand team. This person understands that motion design is storytelling - you're not just animating, you're communicating ideas and driving action. You have a point of view on what makes advertising work and how motion can elevate a brand. The right person has great taste, strong 2D animation fundamentals, and can work across the full production process: from storyboarding through editing and sound. You're comfortable working fast and producing at volume. And you're curious about AI content generation tools - not necessarily an expert, but eager to experiment and learn. We're building a campaign right now that's produced entirely with AI, and we need someone who wants to be part of that. What you’ll do Create motion content for product launches, brand campaigns, social, and events like Canvas • Own projects end-to-end: concept, storyboard, animation, editing, and sound design • Translate complex product features into clear, engaging visual stories • Collaborate with product marketing, corporate marketing, and events teams • Experiment with AI image and video tools to create content at scale • Help evolve Miro's motion design language and maintain consistency across touchpoints What you’ll need 5+ years of professional motion design experience Exceptional 2D animation skills with a portfolio that demonstrates strong taste and timing Experience across the full production cycle - storyboarding, animation, editing, sound Comfort working fast and managing multiple projects with competing deadlines Familiarity with AI image/video generation tools (Runway especially) and eagerness to learn more Self-direction: you take a brief and run with it without heavy oversight What's in it for you We want you to feel supported, connected, and ready to grow. Our global benefits package generally includes equity, a wellbeing benefit, a WFH equipment allowance, and an annual Learning & Development stipend. Join a diverse team where you can do your best work. Full benefits may differ per location. If you would like to learn more about location-specific benefits, please refer to our Global Miro benefits board. #LI-DNIThe reasonably estimated salary range is specific to New York and may not be applicable to other locations. Final compensation and total package components will be based on individual factors such as the candidate's skills, qualifications, and experience. The range for this role is:New York salary range$116,000—$174,000 USDAbout Miro Miro is a visual workspace for innovation that enables distributed teams of any size to build the next big thing. The platform's infinite canvas enables teams to lead engaging workshops and meetings, design products, brainstorm ideas, and more. Miro, co-headquartered in San Francisco and Amsterdam, serves more than 100M users and 250,000 companies collaborate in the Innovation Workspace. Miro was founded in 2011 and currently has more than 1,600 employees in 13 hubs around the world. We are a team of dreamers. We look for individuals who dream big, work hard, and above all stay humble. Collaboration is at the heart of what we do and through our work together we hope to create a supportive, welcoming, and innovative environment. We strive to play as a team to win the world and create a better version of ourselves every day. If this sounds like something that excites you, we want to hear from you! Check out more about life at Miro: Youtube: https://www.youtube.com/@lifeatmiro Blog: https://miro.com/careers/life-at-miro/all/ Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/mirohq/ At Miro, we strive to create and foster an environment of belonging and collaboration across cultural differences. apiJob.title: Visual Communications Designer
apiJob.description: Our StoryPeopleHow we hireTeamsEngineering teamDesign teamProduct teamCustomer experience teamLocationsLife at MiroOpen positionsMenuOur StoryPeopleHow we hireTeamsEngineering teamDesign teamProduct teamCustomer experience teamLocationsLife at MiroOpen positionsHomeOpen positionsVacancyVisual Communications DesignerDepartment: MarketingLocations: Amsterdam, LondonJob Requisition ID: JR001715Apply nowAbout the Team The Miro Brand Creative team is 15 people—designers, producers, project managers, creatives and AI specialists. We're spread across Europe but work with the kind of consistency and closeness you'd expect from a team sat in the same room. High standards, fast pace, and a genuinely varied output that's hard to find at this level elsewhere. About the Role Miro needs a dedicated, senior-level Visual Communications Designer. The immediate priority is Canvas 26—our flagship annual event across four cities—where you'll own the keynote deck end-to-end. But this is a full-time role with a much wider remit: executive and CMO communications, board decks, all-hands, investor materials, and sales enablement. You'll work closely with senior leadership including the CMO, embedded directly in the Miro moments that count. What you’ll do Own the Canvas 26 keynote end-to-end across four cities — San Francisco, London, Tokyo, and Sydney — adapting and iterating as speakers and content evolve Design and deliver all executive and CMO presentations, board decks, all-hands, and sales enablement materials Work directly with senior leadership to shape and refine presentations in real time Transform complex product narratives, strategy, and data into clear, compelling visual stories Build and maintain a presentation system — templates, assets, and standards — that brings consistency across the business Push the craft — typography, layout, data visualisation, animation — and set a new bar for what presentation design looks like at Miro Your work will be seen by thousands of people across four global cities — this is as high visibility as presentation design gets What you’ll need 5+ years of presentation design experience working with C-suite or senior leadership stakeholders A portfolio demonstrating range across keynotes, executive decks, data visualisation, and event content Expert-level Keynote skills including animations, transitions, and build orders Strong working knowledge of Figma, Illustrator, and Photoshop The ability to work fast without losing craft quality, managing multiple priorities simultaneously A strong creative POV and the confidence to defend it—and the good sense to know when to let it go Comfortable with ambiguity, last-minute changes, and the pace that comes with working directly alongside senior leadership What's in it for you We want you to feel supported, connected, and ready to grow. Our global benefits package generally includes equity, a wellbeing benefit, a WFH equipment allowance, and an annual Learning & Development stipend. Join a diverse team where you can do your best work. Full benefits may differ per location. If you would like to learn more about location-specific benefits, please refer to our Global Miro benefits board. #LI-DNIAbout Miro Miro is a visual workspace for innovation that enables distributed teams of any size to build the next big thing. The platform's infinite canvas enables teams to lead engaging workshops and meetings, design products, brainstorm ideas, and more. Miro, co-headquartered in San Francisco and Amsterdam, serves more than 100M users and 250,000 companies collaborate in the Innovation Workspace. Miro was founded in 2011 and currently has more than 1,600 employees in 13 hubs around the world. We are a team of dreamers. We look for individuals who dream big, work hard, and above all stay humble. Collaboration is at the heart of what we do and through our work together we hope to create a supportive, welcoming, and innovative environment. We strive to play as a team to win the world and create a better version of ourselves every day. If this sounds like something that excites you, we want to hear from you! apiJob.title: Senior Product Designer - License Management
apiJob.description: Our StoryPeopleHow we hireTeamsEngineering teamDesign teamProduct teamCustomer experience teamLocationsLife at MiroOpen positionsMenuOur StoryPeopleHow we hireTeamsEngineering teamDesign teamProduct teamCustomer experience teamLocationsLife at MiroOpen positionsHomeOpen positionsVacancySenior Product Designer - License ManagementDepartment: DesignLocations: LondonJob Requisition ID: JR000758Apply nowAbout the Team Miro’s Design team is on a mission to improve the experience of knowledge work, productivity tools, and remote collaboration. We produce best-in-class solutions for a future of work that’s diverse, intuitive, accessible, and enjoyable to use. Maybe that’s why our product is so globally beloved. The Growth team is dedicated to driving new customer acquisition and expanding our user base. A highly-analytical and data-informed team, we work closely with cross-functional partners to design and implement strategies that fuel Miro's growth. Our focus is on creating data-driven, user-centric designs that balance business goals with exceptional user experiences. About the Role The Senior Product Designer for the License Management team will design foundational systems at the intersection of user experience, scale, and monetisation. You will tackle complex, high-leverage challenges that shape how millions of customers access and pay for Miro, as well as designing and shipping experiments that drive FR and Guest users towards paid seats, and activate paid seats to drive MAUs. What you’ll do Lead the design of growth-focused features and experiments from ideation through launch, iterating based on insights from user research and quantitative data Collaborate with PMs, Engineers, Data Analysts, and other designers to define hypotheses, design experiments, and measure impact on growth metrics Translate data insights and user feedback into thoughtful, intuitive design solutions that help drive customer growth, product usage, and revenue Apply your understanding of SaaS growth concepts, including customer acquisition, activation, retention, and monetization to your design work for product-led growth and enterprise users Balance business objectives with user needs, ensuring both are reflected in your process and deliverables Advocate for high design quality and consistency, collaborating with the design system team to ensure solutions align with our broader product experience Communicate design rationale and solutions clearly to stakeholders, proactively seeking feedback and alignment Learn from and collaborate with a talented team of designers, sharing feedback and growing your craftStay current on growth design best practices, experimentation methodologies, and industry trends to inform and inspire your work Explore using AI tools to speed up validation, design discovery, and prototyping What you’ll need 5+ years of product design experience, ideally in B2B SaaS or growth-focused roles, with a portfolio showcasing your design thinking and impact Experience designing and running data-driven experiments, and iterating based on results Experience working on projects that have a direct impact on product usage or revenue Good understanding of core growth concepts for SaaS products and platforms (e.g., user acquisition, onboarding, retention). Ability to balance business goals with user needs, delivering high-quality, user-centric designs Strong collaboration skills with cross-functional partners, including PMs, Engineers, Data Analysts, and other designers Proficiency in Figma and prototyping tools; experience with analytics platforms is a plus Clear communication and openness to feedback, with a desire to learn and grow as part of a team Growth mindset and a passion for experimentation Experience using AI tools to accelerate validation, prototyping, or design discovery Experience with enterprise applications and services is a plus What's in it for you We want you to feel supported, connected, and ready to grow. apiJob.title: Senior Content Designer
apiJob.description: Our StoryPeopleHow we hireTeamsEngineering teamDesign teamProduct teamCustomer experience teamLocationsLife at MiroOpen positionsMenuOur StoryPeopleHow we hireTeamsEngineering teamDesign teamProduct teamCustomer experience teamLocationsLife at MiroOpen positionsHomeOpen positionsVacancySenior Content DesignerDepartment: DesignLocations: LondonJob Requisition ID: JR001613Apply nowAbout the Team We have a small but mighty content design team at Miro, with just three people across multiple key initiatives. This means we must be flexible, resilient and good at prioritising projects where we can have the biggest impact! We're an integral part of the Design org, shaping the user experience for some of our most impactful initiatives. We operate within a hybrid model, embedding deeply within high-priority cross-functional teams to craft user-centered content that guides and delights our users, and offering light support to lower priority work. About the Role We’re looking for a talented Content Designer and AI whizz to join our team and contribute directly to high-impact projects that elevate the Miro experience. This role will sit within our AI Canvas team, specifically working on enhancing our AI Workflow experiences. You'll partner with senior leadership across product, design, and engineering, and build the frameworks that help other teams create consistent AI content at scale. You’ll also be a key player in shaping the content design discipline at Miro, collaborating closely with product managers, designers, researchers, and product marketers and delivering content that enhances usability and drives adoption. This is an exciting opportunity to make a meaningful impact on our product and be part of growing the maturity of our content design practice. What you’ll do Work with product, UX, and engineering teams to help shape the product vision and create user-focused content for high-priority AI features and initiatives – ensuring clarity and consistency across the product Design and develop prompts, UI copy and error messages to help our users adopt Sidekicks and Flows and get high quality results when using them Plan, develop and deliver conversational content that guides users effectively, reduces cognitive load, and aligns with Miro’s voice and tone guidelines Contribute to the refinement and application of Miro’s content design guidelines, particularly focused on refining our AI principles and guidance which will ultimately need to live in our design system and MCPs Collaborate with design and UX research to conduct user research, test content hypotheses, and iterate solutions based on data and feedback Participate in design and product reviews, providing clear rationale for your content design decisions Advocate for the value of content design, helping to integrate the discipline into the broader product development process On a typical day you'll be: Writing and testing system prompts, then debugging the model Defining evaluation criteria with engineering and reviewing AI outputs against quality benchmarks Shaping conversation flows and interaction patterns in design crits and tiger teams Running through edge cases to figure out where the AI breaks and how to recover gracefully Creating conversation starters and prompt suggestions that teach users what's actually possible What you’ll need 6+ years of experience in content design or UX writing, with a strong background in creating content for digital products A strong portfolio showcasing your ability to create clear, user-focused content and demonstrating the impact on user experience and business goals Proven experience working closely with cross-functional teams, including product management, UX design, and engineering, to deliver seamless content solutions Deep understanding of UX writing principles, information architecture, and user-centered design methodologies Familiarity with AI products, conversation design, or prompt engineering (you don't need to be an engineer, but you should be comfortable getting hands-on with how AI systems behave) Ability to align product content strategy with business objectives, balancing user needs with technical feasibility Strong written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to articulate the value of content design and collaborate effectively across diverse teams A proactive and flexible approach, comfortable working autonomously in a fast-paced, sometimes ambiguous environment and managing multiple priorities What's in it for you We want you to feel supported, connected, and ready to grow. Our global benefits package generally includes equity, a wellbeing benefit, a WFH equipment allowance, and an annual Learning & Development stipend. We operate in a fast-paced environment and get to have a direct impact on the millions of users who rely on Miro to collaborate and create the next big thing. As a part of our Product Design team, you will contribute to our design process, culture, and experience principles and set new standards for design quality. Learn more about the colorful design team and our culture: https://miro.design/ About the Role The Miro Graduate Product Design Programme is designed to help new designers launch their careers in design, research or content writing. In our 12-month programme, you'll gain hands-on experience working on real-world projects. You'll also be mentored by experienced designers, researchers, and content writers, who will help you develop your skills and career. As a graduate product designer at Miro, you will focus on learning, growing and bringing a new fresh perspective to the Miro experience. Your goal will be to explore the customer experience, identify opportunities, explore the fields of product design, UXR, and content design, and deliver high-quality solutions but most of all to learn and grow as a new designer at Miro and find your place from Product Design, Research, Content Design or maybe somewhere else. What you’ll do Explore the end-to-end design process, from discovery research to effective problem framing, ideation, co-creation, prototyping, and user testing, into high-fidelity designs Identify where you need to dive deep, and running your own research and collaborate closely with UXR and analytics to turn insights into high-quality outcomes Work not just on designs, but the story we tell and work closely with content design to elevate the product beyond visuals. Understand the success of your solutions with the team and iterate on them closely with engineering partners to ensure solid implementation in production. Understand the competitive landscape, and using qualitative and quantitative methods hypothesize new solutions and improvements to the Miro products. Bring delight to Miro users through customer centric solutions implemented thought high-quality interaction design and creative visuals.. What you’ll need Graduate in 2025-6 in Bachelor’s or Master’s in interaction design, industrial design, creative design, HCI, or a related design field Strong drive to learn, and absorb knowledge from your team, and ability to take and utilize this knowledge in your work AI-First Proficiency - We’re building for an AI-first future, and we’re looking for graduates who aren’t just using AI—they’re mastering it. Effective written and verbal communication skills Be available to start a full time position for our 12 month programme from August 2026 Be able to commute to our office in Amsterdam or Berlin What's in it for you We want you to feel supported, connected, and ready to grow. Our global benefits package generally includes equity, a wellbeing benefit, a WFH equipment allowance, and an annual Learning & Development stipend. Join a diverse team where you can do your best work. Full benefits may differ per location. If you would like to learn more about location-specific benefits, please refer to our Global Miro benefits board.A note on location:We’d love to hear from you! Please keep in mind that we are currently unable to provide relocation assistance for this role. If you are applying from outside the Netherlands or Berlin, you would need to be prepared to manage your own relocation and any associated costs.About Miro Miro is a visual workspace for innovation that enables distributed teams of any size to build the next big thing. The platform's infinite canvas enables teams to lead engaging workshops and meetings, design products, brainstorm ideas, and more. Miro, co-headquartered in San Francisco and Amsterdam, serves more than 100M users and 250,000 companies collaborate in the Innovation Workspace. Miro was founded in 2011 and currently has more than 1,600 employees in 13 hubs around the world. We are a team of dreamers. We look for individuals who dream big, work hard, and above all stay humble. Collaboration is at the heart of what we do and through our work together we hope to create a supportive, welcoming, and innovative environment. We strive to play as a team to win the world and create a better version of ourselves every day. If this sounds like something that excites you, we want to hear from you! Check out more about life at Miro: Youtube: https://www.youtube.com/@lifeatmiro Blog: https://miro.com/careers/life-at-miro/all/ Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/mirohq/ At Miro, we strive to create and foster an environment of belonging and collaboration across cultural differences. Miro’s mission — Empower teams to create the next big thing — is how we think about our product, people, and culture. We believe that creating big things requires diverse and inclusive teams. Diversity invites all talent with different demography, identities and styles to step in, and inclusion invites them to step closer together. Every day, we are working to build a more diverse Miro, cultivate a sense of belonging for future and current Mironeers around the world, and foster an environment where everyone can collaborate and embrace differences. apiJob.title: Full time Coordinator, Sales Planning & Operations - Global Formats & Brand Licensing Sony Pictures Television London, United Kingdom (Hybrid)
apiJob.description:
Please note that this role is based in theUnited Kingdom. To enable us to meet the statutory and regulatory obligations of the United Kingdom immigration system, you must have the appropriate immigration permission needed to work and reside in the United Kingdom for the duration of the employment.

Sony Pictures Entertainment is one of the industry’s leading content providers, producing and distributing worldwide in every genre and for every platform. In addition to managing one of the industry’s largest libraries of award-winning feature films, television shows and formats, SPE is home to a thriving global content business, operating a robust portfolio of wholly owned and joint-venture production companies across the U.S., Europe, Latin America, and Asia Pacific, as well as linear and digital channels around the world.

We have an exciting new opportunity to support our Global Formats and Brand Licensing team based in London. The formats team is focused on growing the reach of our Unscripted and Scripted format product, such as Who Wants To Be A Millionaire, Dragons’ Den and Raid the Cage; by launching and maintaining local adaptations in as many territories as possible. The Brand Licensing team extends the licensing of our IP across consumer products, mobile games, live events and digital/social media channels.

What you'll do:

Brand Licensing Deals Project (1st 6 months)

  • Source all Brand Licensing revenue contracts and amendments and save them in the central SharePoint repository
  • Enter all Brand Licensing revenue deals into S2F and process all unbilled revenue, in collaboration with the local Finance team
  • Update project tracker and communicate progress to all key stakeholders
  • Create process documentation in conjunction with EMEA Central Sales Planning team to document new workflow and deal entry process for all Brand Licensing revenue deals
  • Design reports to ensure VP, Brand Management and Interactives Strategy is kept up to date on the status of all deals

Deal Management & Administration

  • Set up and maintain deals in Sales & Rights Management systems (RPM) and (S2F - Sales to Finance)
  • Manage contractual obligations and ensure all deadlines are met as detailed in the contracts
  • Assist in deal execution, working closely with Sales and Legal on contract drafting, documentation, and execution.
  • Track the workflow of contract, amendment, notification processes for every deal, client schedules, availability lists and ad hoc reports
  • Liaise with Finance on customer checks, invoicing/payment issues, and audit queries
  • Liaise with clients to chase outstanding reports
  • Ensure all Sales Planning Procedures are upheld by seeking appropriate reviews and Approvals
  • Work closely with Sales and Planning in the territory to ensure clear information flow between local and central teams

Process Optimisation

  • Act as key point of contact for Formats and Brand Licensing deal management, guiding Sales planning processes to Sales and Planning teams.
  • Ensure process documentation for formats and Brand Licensing deals entry is kept up to date.
  • Build relationships with LATAM and APAC teams to ensure they are across the current processes and are ensuring systems reflect the latest deals and forecasts.

Reporting

  • Ownership of regular and ad hoc reporting for Sales, providing clear visibility on performance, pipeline, and opportunities.
  • Work closely with Senior Manager to prepare Global Formats budget presentation, including data consolidation, analysis, and clear commercial commentary.
  • Ongoing maintenance and development of the Print Opportunity Database.

Forecasting & Planning

  • Maintain/update forecasts in RPM; process changes in a timely manner and keep Senior Manager and Sales Teams informed of updates regularly
  • Assist with variance analysis on product, deals and/or market assumptions to support Sales in preparing the forecast
  • Proactive follow-up during year-end and other critical periods to ensure deals, forecasts, and budget reports are accurate and complete.

What you have:

  • Educated to a minimum undergraduate degree level preferable (but not necessarily)
  • Ideally, 1-2 years’ experience in a similar environment
  • Strong IT skills, in particular Excel (Pivot Tables & Formulae), and must be comfortable in learning new systems
  • Fluency in English is essential
  • Highly numerate with excellent attention to detail
  • Problem Solving & Decision making – demonstrates an ability to address root causes of problems, identify alternatives and propose solutions.
  • Able to use complex information to provide summaries with clear and meaningful insights
  • Managing priorities and competing deadlines during busy periods
  • Proactive and adaptable – anticipate needs in different situations and take appropriate action with guidance and on own initiative
  • Excellent communication skills
  • Team player who is willing and able to assist colleagues

How we take care of you:

  • Competitive salary, with annual bonus eligibility.
  • A choice of comprehensive health plan options that fit your lifestyle, including private medical insurance.
  • Rest and recharge during a week off during the winter holidays, in addition to the 25 days of paid annual leave.
  • Participate in extensive learning & development opportunities at all levels, including curated instructor-led classes and high-impact online resources.
  • Build your community by joining our Employee Business Resource Groups, and/or Sony Pictures Action – our racial equity and inclusion strategy.
  • Access to an employee online store filled with a variety of discounted Sony products.
  • Watch the newest movies and TV shows at our exclusive employee screenings at work.
  • Entitlement to apply for an interest-free season ticket loan.
  • Automatic enrolment in the Company’s Pension Plan
  • Free counselling and advisory service.
  • You will be automatically enrolled in the Company’s Life Insurance and Group Income Protection Plans, subject to any applicable service requirements.
  • The opportunity to attend various social events in our state-of-the-art offices in Paddington.
  • In addition to the above, we have a range of other optional discounted benefits that are reviewed regularly (e.g., gym memberships, travel insurance, activity passes, car leasing, experience days and many more!)

If you require any reasonable adjustments with any part of the recruitment process, including the application or interview process, please contact us atuk_-_peopleandorganisation@spe.sony.com.Please put Reasonable Adjustment Request in the subject line of the email.

Sony Pictures does not allow audio recording, video recording or use of AI note-taking tools during interviews. Candidates requiring these tools as an accommodation during an interview should submit a reasonable adjustment request at the point they are invited to interview.

Sony Pictures Entertainment is committed to equal opportunity in all its employment practices, policies and procedures. No worker or potential worker will therefore receive less favourable treatment due to their race, age, creed, sexual orientation, colour, nationality, ethnic origin, disability, religion, gender, marital status or Trade Union membership (if applicable).

#LI-TD1

apiJob.title: Full time Sr Manager, FP&A - Motion Picture Group Corporate Culver City, California (Hybrid)
apiJob.description:
Sony Pictures Entertainment (SPE) is a leading creator and distributor of entertainment products, services, and technology. SPE’s global operations encompass motion picture production and distribution, television programming and syndication, home video acquisitions and distribution, operation of studio facilities, development of new entertainment technologies, and distribution of filmed entertainment worldwide.

Currently, SPE is seeking a Senior Manager of Divisional Finance for the Motion Picture Group (MPG) in the Culver City, CA office. This position will assist in driving comprehensive planning and strategic decision-making activity across all feature film operations. This position is responsible for preparing P&L and Cash Flow forecasts, managing financial models, and partnering with finance and operating personnel to deliver actionable analytics that enhance performance and accuracy. The ideal candidate will bring technical expertise, strong leadership, analytical, and communication skills, and a proactive approach to refining the forecasting processes.

Key Responsibilities

Core Financial Planning & Analysis

  • Supervise and assist in preparation of annual Budgets, Forecasts, Mid-Range Plans

  • Collaborate with various teams, including MPG Business Development, on greenlight/film model updates, ultimate profitability and by-title performance tracking

  • Coordinate with lines of business (Marketing, Distribution, Production, Corporate Finance) and all Theatrical Distribution Labels (MPG, Cruncyroll) on product/content (for upcoming 3 years) and cash flow assumptions

  • Run impairment risks on current releases and/or on 3rd party co-financing deals, as needed for forecasts; prepare risk analyses and supplemental schedules for the accounting group (GFO) and external auditors, as requested

  • Oversee preparation of Consolidated 3-Year Cash Flows and Balance Sheet forecasts; ensure working capital targets are met

  • For the Consolidated Cash Flow forecast, prepare the executive-level presentation, including variance analyses, for DCFO and CFO review

  • Lend subject matter expertise to staff on all functions to continue optimizing workflow efficiencies and accuracy

Advanced Analytics & Modeling

  • Perform box office analysis on new releases, industry comparatives, by-title market analysis, and various other metrics to provide financial guidance to management

  • Execute regression analysis and other advanced modeling techniques to enhance forecasting timing models on certain P&L and Cash flows

  • Evaluate Net Present Value on certain revenue and cost items, including content valuations, obligations, revenue recognition (ASC 606)

  • Oversee modeling updates, tracking, and preparation of supporting schedules on slate financing deals, as needed; collaborate with MPG Business Development and GFO

Governance, Compliance & Cross-Functional Collaboration

  • Assist in drafting SOX controls and ensure compliance with all financial controls; coordinate with GFO and Technical Accounting

  • With staff, coordinate with participations group to review statements issued to participants and investors and ensure payment accuracy

  • Assist as liaison with technical accounting on new deal structures, ensuring models align with IFRS-compliant treatments

  • Approve division ultimates and third-party invoices, e.g., with TV or streaming providers, as part of film model ultimate workflows

  • Partner with Financial Reporting team (GFO) for P&L and Cash monthly performance comparisons to plan

  • Support system enhancements and user testing for forecasting efficiencies.

Education & Certifications

  • BS/BA degree with accounting/finance/economics focus required

  • CPA and/or MBA/Masters degree strongly preferred

Experience & Skills

  • 5+ years of progressive experience in financial planning, modeling, and accounting; entertainment/media industry experience strongly preferred

  • 1-2 years of managerial experience, including managing, developing, and leading staff

  • Deep expertise in technical modeling and indirect and direct cash flow forecasting, working capital, and balance sheet analyses

  • Advanced Excel and PowerPoint skills; SAP or similar ERP experience preferred

  • Experience with enterprise planning systems (e.g. SAP BPC, SmartView, etc.) and business intelligence tools (e.g. Power BI, Tableau, others)

  • Knowledge of IFRS and U.S. GAAP principles; SOX compliance experience required

  • Strong communication skills with the ability to present complex financial insights to executives

  • Collaborative leadership style; able to lead cross-functional initiatives and mentor team members

  • Highly detail-oriented, organized, and able to manage competing priorities in a fast-paced, deadline driven environment

  • Demonstrated ability to improve processes, challenge the status quo, and drive efficiency through automation or analytics

  • Possess a high level of intellectual curiosity, focus on generating results and exhibit the highest personal and professional standards of integrity and ethics

apiJob.title: 3D Animation Artist - Unpublished R&D Product (Contract)
apiJob.description:
Art3D Animation Artist - Unpublished R&D Product (Contract)Guangzhou, ChinaJob Id: 6010职位概述 作为 3D 动作设计师（Contractor），你将加入我们的美术团队，负责为游戏角色、生物或道具制作高质量的关键帧动画（Keyframe Animation）。我们需要一位执行力强、基本功扎实的动画师。你不需要负责复杂的动捕数据清洗或底层的动画系统搭建，但你需要能独立承担一个角色或模块的动画量产工作，确保动画在引擎中表现流畅，并符合项目的艺术风格与玩法需求。 你将与玩法策划、技术美术紧密合作，通过动画赋予角色生命力，为玩家提供极致的打击感与操作反馈。 核心职责 高质量动画制作： 根据需求文档和分镜，使用 Maya 制作高质量的 3D 角色动画（包括但不限于：待机、移动、战斗技能、受击、死亡等）。 风格化表现： 深刻理解并还原项目的艺术风格（例如：美式卡通、强打击感、夸张变形等），确保动作设计符合角色性格（Acting）。 引擎集成与调试： 将动画资源导入游戏引擎（如 Unreal/Unity 或自研引擎），配置动画混合树（Blend Trees）、状态机（State Machines）基础设置，并能够在引擎内预览和验收效果。 玩法手感打磨： 与玩法策划紧密配合，通过调整动画的节奏（Timing/Spacing）和帧数，优化游戏的操作手感和响应速度（Gameplay Feel）。 Bug修复与优化： 负责维护和修复现有动画资源中的穿插、滑步、抖动等问题，确保交付资源的稳定性。 任职要求 经验要求： 3年以上游戏行业 3D 动画制作经验，至少参与过一款完整的游戏项目开发。 专业技能： 精通 Autodesk Maya 的动画模块。 深刻理解动画 12项基本法则（如挤压拉伸、预备动作、跟随动作等），尤其是在人体力学（Body Mechanics）和重量感表现上有扎实功底。 擅长 纯手K（Keyframe） 动画制作，能够处理风格化（Stylized）的角色动态。 沟通与协作： 具备良好的沟通能力（P2核心能力），能够准确理解反馈意见并快速迭代。 能够主动与上下游（策划/模型/特效）对齐需求，预判潜在的制作风险。 作品集： 必须提供 Demo Reel，展示涵盖肢体运动、战斗动作和表演片段的高质量作品。 加分项 引擎经验： 熟悉 Unreal Engine 或 Unity 的动画蓝图/系统。 技术理解： 了解基础的绑定（Rigging）知识，能进行简单的蒙皮（Skinning）修复或给 TA 提供有效的绑定反馈。 多风格适应： 既能驾驭写实风格，也能处理夸张卡通风格。 第一人称经验： 有 FPS（第一人称射击）武器与手部动画制作经验者优先。 用工性质：与专业人事服务公司FESCO Adecco签订劳动合同.Apply

apiJob.title: Content Producer - LoL Esports
apiJob.description:
Production GroupContent Producer - LoL EsportsSeoul, KoreaJob Id: REQ-0009768Riot Games was established in 2006 by avid gamers who believe that player-focused game development can result in great games. In 2009, Riot released its debut title League of Legends to critical and player acclaim. Players form the foundation of our community and it’s for them that we continue to evolve and improve the League of Legends experience, and beyond! We celebrated the 15th year of LoL esports at the 2025 World Championship and we hope to share many more great experiences to our players through esports. We’re looking for passionate professionals who share the same goal to make LoL esports even greater. That's where you come in. As a Content Producer - LoL Esports, you will report into Content Production Lead. You’ll be our Esports video visionary, creating and shepherding technically excellent, fun-heightened, player-approved, gameplay video content from conception to delivery, improving the quality bar for the Content Services Group in Korea. As a Rioter in Seoul, you’ll join a tight-knit group of exceptionally bright-minded individuals who share our prioritization of player experience, rejection of convention, and passion for gaming. Riot Games Korea is dedicated to creating tailored player experiences that show local players they aren’t lost in translation. Responsibilities: Coordinate with a variety of Esports technical, creative, and live broadcast teams, as well as other partners, to guide Esports gameplay video projects through post-production and deliver best-in-class industry results. Infuse creative and planning meetings with fresh ideas for new, electrifying Esports content that’ll blow players away. Take ownership of the defined creative vision and shape content from pre-production to delivery. Evaluate our Esports content, presenting new ways to improve our technical and editorial skills . Ability to manage stakeholder reviews, maintaining detailed communications throughout a video’s process from concept to delivery. Required Qualifications: Experienced: You have 5+ years of work experience as a producer in gaming, sports, or entertainment content production, with a proven ability to create and execute project plans and schedules. You bring strong knowledge of diverse project types (web, mobile, video, brand, strategic consulting) and the capability to develop and produce platform-specific content in a rapidly evolving content ecosystem. A creative story teller: you have the skill and the passion to craft compelling, authentic, and fresh narratives for Esports audiences. You possess production & project management experience with a reel or links demonstrating strong creative video content. A team player: you're open to performing any task, whether big or small; you're willing to teach and learn, lead, and follow. A culture-savvy enthusiast: Whether it’s NFL, NBA, MLB, or EPL, you have a broad interest and passion not only in sports but also entertainment, and cultural trends across the broader arts and culture landscape.You have a strong understanding of why audiences connect with certain cultural phenomena and apply those insights to content planning and storytelling. For this role, you'll find success through craft expertise, a collaborative spirit, and decision-making that prioritizes the delight of players. We will be looking at your past studies, experience, and your personal relationship with games. If you embody player empathy and care about players' experiences, this could be your role!Apply

apiJob.title: Content Producer - Riftbound (Contract)
apiJob.description:
PublishingContent Producer - Riftbound (Contract)Shanghai, ChinaJob Id: 6019Riot Games was established in 2006 by entrepreneurial gamers who believe that player-focused game development can result in great games. In 2009, Riot released its debut title ---- League of Legends to critical and player acclaim. As the most played video game in the world, over 100 million play every month. Players form the foundation of our community and it’s for them that we continue to evolve and improve the experience.​ We’re looking for humble but ambitious, razor-sharp professionals who can teach us a thing or two. We promise to return the favor. Like us, you take play seriously; you’re passionate about games. We embrace those who see things differently, aren’t afraid to experiment, and who have a healthy disregard for constraints.​ That's where you come in.​ As Content Producer – Publishing, Riftbound, you will report to the Regional Publishing Lead. Your role will involve translating our strategic goals into creative campaigns that surprise and delight our players. Partnering internally and externally across various disciplines, you will ensure a smooth creative production process and assets management process. Your awareness and transparent communication will provide you with a comprehensive understanding of our campaign requirements, allowing you to advocate for their needs and ensure they are fulfilled.​ Responsibilities​ Assist in developing and executing content strategies aligned with team goals Work with publishers or vendors to ensure timely production and delivery of high-quality content; coordinate with publishers or vendors to ensure timely production and delivery of high-quality content Monitor and analyze content performance metrics to support optimization of future content Collaborate closely with the team to ensure content supports overall business objectives.​ Maintain a content calendar to ensure consistent publication schedules Participate in content audits to ensure quality and consistency Provide day-to-day feedback on the visual design production process and ensure accuracy in deliveries.​ Knowledge & Skills​ Expertise in project and production management, with proven experience in content management and strategy, encompassing all facets of campaign, video, and art production.​ Strong attention to detail with excellent writing, editing, and communication skills to evaluate and ensure high standards in content output.​ Proficient in managing a high volume of creative and art marketing assets, adept at maintaining consistency and quality across multiple projects Understanding of the visual design production process.​ Highly proficient communication skills and capacity.​ Required Qualifications​ At least 3 years of experience in the relevant fields, with a proven track record in delivering marketing campaigns and managing art assets Fluent in spoken English and Mandarin.​ Basic understanding of advertising, production, and/or project management Passionate or experience in game marketing.​ Desired Qualifications​ Interest or experience in game marketing, card games or TCG preferably Proficiency in Adobe Creative Suite or similar tools for delivery validation.​ Expertise in project management tools and digital asset management systems.​ Card game collector/TCG player or fans with strong familiarity of the IP/ community. Employment Type: employment contract with FESCO Adecco, a professional personnel company in China.Apply

apiJob.title: Director of Music Affairs / General ManagerEntertainmentEntertainmentLos Angeles, USA
apiJob.description:
EntertainmentDirector of Music Affairs / General ManagerLos Angeles, USAJob Id: REQ-0009639Riot Games Music (RGM) has an in-house label and publisher that creates, licenses, releases, and manages a diverse catalog of music content for Riot Games' universe of titles and virtual artists, including VALORANT, League of Legends, K/DA, and Pentakill. We are dedicated to delivering world-class music experiences that deepen player immersion and expand the creative boundaries of gaming. Riot Games Music is seeking a seasoned and dynamic executive to take on the role of Director of Music Affairs and General Manager. This individual will be the driving force behind the recorded music and publishing efforts of RGM, overseeing all label operations and strategy. This is a critical leadership position requiring a deep understanding of the global music industry, exceptional business acumen, and a passion for managing and mentoring high-performing teams. Responsibilities: Label and Publishing Strategy & Operations: Develop and execute the overall strategy for RGM's recorded music releases and publishing catalog, ensuring alignment with Riot Games’ product, brand and content goals. Manage the operations for all label and publishing activities, driving revenue growth and operational efficiency. Establish and maintain best-in-class partnerships and processes for all aspects of the music business. Business Affairs & External Partnerships: Oversee all Business Affairs functions, including deal negotiation and execution for artist, producer, writer, and third-party partner agreements in partnership with Riot’s legal teams Manage existing and forge new strategic external partnerships with Tencent, major labels, distributors, PROs (Performing Rights Organizations), CMOs (Collective Management Organizations), and other key music industry stakeholders. Ensure RGM is compliant with all global music copyright, licensing, and regulatory requirements. Marketing & Partnerships: Direct the strategy for all RGM releases, working closely with internal Riot marketing teams and external partners to maximize global reach and fan engagement. Manage and support artist partnerships at the highest levels of the global industry to drive catalog growth and Riot’s products goals Royalty, Catalog, and Rights Administration: Supervise the administration of the RGM music catalog, including metadata management, content delivery, and registration with global rights organizations. Oversee all royalty accounting and reporting functions to ensure timely and accurate payments to artists, writers, and partners. Team Leadership & Management: Act as a General Manager, leading, mentoring, and developing a team of young professionals across various label functions (A&R, Operations, Marketing, Business Affairs). Foster a collaborative, innovative, and high-performance culture within the RGM team. Required Qualifications: 10-15 years of progressive leadership experience within the recorded music industry, with significant time spent in a label management or executive business affairs role. Deep expertise in music publishing, intellectual property law, and digital distribution. Experience in major Asian music markets including China and Korea Proven track record of success in negotiating complex music deals and managing multi-million dollar budgets. Extensive network of relationships within the global music, technology, and entertainment industries. Track record of implementing cutting edge tools to drive innovation and operational efficiencies Preferred Qualifications: Experience managing music operations for a video game company or adjacent media/entertainment property. Working knowledge of the gaming and esports ecosystem. Demonstrated ability to thrive in a fast-paced, creatively-driven, and entrepreneurial environment. For this role, you'll find success through craft expertise, a collaborative spirit, and decision-making that prioritizes the delight of players. We will be looking at your past studies, experience, and your personal relationship with games. If you embody player empathy and care about players' experiences, this could be your role! Our Perks: Riot focuses on work/life balance, shown by our open paid time off policy and other perks such as flexible work schedules. We offer medical, dental, and life insurance, parental leave for you, your spouse/domestic partner, and children, and a 401k with company match. Check out our benefits pages for more information. At Riot Games, we put players first. That mission drives every decision in our quest to create games and experiences that make it better to be a player. Whether you’re working directly on a new player-facing experience or you’re supporting the company as a whole, everyone at Riot is part of our mission. And just like in our games, we’re better when we work together. Our goal is to create collaborative teams where you are empowered to bring your unique perspective everyday. If that sounds like the kind of place you want to work, we’re looking forward to your application. It’s our policy to provide equal employment opportunity for all applicants and members of Riot Games, Inc. Riot Games makes reasonable accommodations for handicapped and disabled Rioters and does not unlawfully discriminate on the basis of race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, national origin, age, handicap, veteran status, marital status, criminal history, or any other category protected by applicable federal and state law. We consider for employment all qualified applicants, including those with criminal histories, in a manner consistent with applicable federal, state and local law, including the California Fair Chance Act, the City of Los Angeles Fair Chance Initiative for Hiring Ordinance, the Los Angeles County Fair Chance Ordinance for Employers, the San Francisco Fair Chance Ordinance, and the Washington Fair Chance Act. Per the Los Angeles County Fair Chance Ordinance, the following core duties may create a basis for disqualifying candidates with relevant criminal histories: Safeguarding confidential and sensitive Company data Communication with others, including Rioters and third parties such as vendors, and/or players, including minors Accessing Company assets, secure digital systems, and networks Ensuring a safe interactive environment for players and other Rioters These duties are directly related to essential operations, safety, trust, and compliance obligations within our organization. Please note that job duties may evolve based on business needs and additional responsibilities may be assigned as necessary to maintain operational efficiency and security. (Los Angeles Only) Base salary range between $221,900.00 - $333,900.00 USD + incentive compensation + equity + 401K with company match + medical, dental, vision, and life insurance + short and long-term disability + open PTO.Apply

apiJob.title: Game Designer III, Competitive Systems- VALORANT
apiJob.description:
DesignGame Designer III, Competitive Systems- VALORANTLos Angeles, USAAdditional Locations: Mercer Island, USAJob Id: REQ-0008434As a Senior Game Designer, you will design, tweak, and optimize the player experience. Your analytical skills, player insight, and creativity aid in crafting in- and out-of-game experiences that engage and surprise players. You will collaborate with interdisciplinary teams and work with every tool at hand to innovate and iterate player-focused designs that'll delight millions around the world. VALORANT is Riot’s take on a character-based tactical shooter (for more info on VALORANT click here!). As a Game Designer III on the Competitive Systems team, you’ll design and deliver world-class competitive and ranked systems that celebrate mastery, fairness, and player growth. You’ll develop a deep understanding of what drives competitive players and design systems that are both strategically sound and deeply satisfying. You’ll also craft solutions that address diverse player experiences across platforms, ensuring competitive integrity and engagement for all. This role reports to the Lead Metasystems Designer. Responsibilities: Design and evolve competitive and ranked systems that reward player skill, growth, and long-term investment in VALORANT. Develop and support matchmaking systems that ensure fair and balanced competition. Collaborate with cross-functional teams to bring new features from concept to live deployment. Identify and explore new design opportunities in partnership with Product Managers and Design Leadership. Create multiple design solutions with clear articulation of trade-offs, risks, and alignment with product and player goals. Playtest VALORANT regularly, providing feedback to improve existing systems and prototype new ideas. Required Qualifications: 5+ years of professional Game Design experience. Shipped or contributed to at least one AAA+ online competitive title. Demonstrated ability to design and iterate on complex systems with a clear understanding of player impact and downstream effects. Strong communication skills with proven ability to articulate design rationale and decision frameworks to team members. Desired Qualifications: Deep passion and understanding of competitive online games and their player communities. Experience developing matchmaking, ranked, or progression systems. Familiarity with online shooters, especially VALORANT or tactical shooters. Ability to interpret and learn from charged or emotional player feedback to improve the product experience. Experience balancing competitive integrity with engaging and rewarding gameplay systems. Experience developing across multiple platforms (PC, Mobile, Console). For this role, you'll find success through craft expertise and a collaborative spirit that prioritizes the delight of players. We will look at your past studies and experience, but for this role, we also look for dedicated people with a personal relationship with games. If you embody player empathy and care about players' experiences, this is the role for you! Our Perks: Riot has a focus on work/life balance, shown by our open paid time off policy, in addition to other perks such as flexible work schedules. We offer medical, dental, and life insurance, parental leave for you, your spouse/domestic partner and children, and a 401k with company match. Check out our benefits pages for more information. At Riot Games, we put players first. That mission drives every decision in our quest to create games and experiences that make it better to be a player. Whether you’re working directly on a new player-facing experience or you’re supporting the company as a whole, everyone at Riot is part of our mission. And just like in our games, we’re better when we work together. Our goal is to create collaborative teams where you are empowered to bring your unique perspective everyday. If that sounds like the kind of place you want to work, we’re looking forward to your application. It’s our policy to provide equal employment opportunity for all applicants and members of Riot Games, Inc. Riot Games makes reasonable accommodations for handicapped and disabled Rioters and does not unlawfully discriminate on the basis of race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, national origin, age, handicap, veteran status, marital status, criminal history, or any other category protected by applicable federal and state law. We consider for employment all qualified applicants, including those with criminal histories, in a manner consistent with applicable federal, state and local law, including the California Fair Chance Act, the City of Los Angeles Fair Chance Initiative for Hiring Ordinance, the Los Angeles County Fair Chance Ordinance for Employers, the San Francisco Fair Chance Ordinance, and the Washington Fair Chance Act. Per the Los Angeles County Fair Chance Ordinance, the following core duties may create a basis for disqualifying candidates with relevant criminal histories: Safeguarding confidential and sensitive Company data Communication with others, including Rioters and third parties such as vendors, and/or players, including minors Accessing Company assets, secure digital systems, and networks Ensuring a safe interactive environment for players and other Rioters These duties are directly related to essential operations, safety, trust, and compliance obligations within our organization. Please note that job duties may evolve based on business needs and additional responsibilities may be assigned as necessary to maintain operational efficiency and security. (Los Angeles Only) Base salary range between $150,000.00 - $209,600.00 USD + incentive compensation + equity + 401K with company match + medical, dental, vision, and life insurance + short and long-term disability + open PTO.(Mercer Island only) Base salary range between $150,000.00 - $209,600.00 USD + incentive compensation + equity + 401K with company match + medical, dental, vision, and life insurance + short and long-term disability + open PTO.Apply

apiJob.title: Game Designer, Combat - Unpublished R&D Product (Contract)
apiJob.description:
DesignGame Designer, Combat - Unpublished R&D Product (Contract)Shanghai, ChinaJob Id: 5920作为一名游戏设计师，你将负责设计、调整并优化玩家体验。你出色的分析能力、对玩家的洞察力以及创造力将共同打造游戏内外令人惊喜且具有吸引力的玩家体验。你将与跨学科团队合作，充分运用各种工具，持续迭代和优化以玩家为核心的设计，致力于让全球数百万玩家感到满意与喜爱。 在研发（R&D）团队中担任战斗游戏设计师，负责一个早期阶段的产品，你将参与揭示 Riot 下一款重点项目的方向。你将与其他设计师、艺术家和工程师密切协作，优化和迭代以玩家为中心的设计，致力于创造突破性的游戏体验。由于团队规模仍较小，该岗位需要大量实操工作，亲自创建和调校战斗系统/机制。你应能适应目标与限制主要由团队驱动的不确定性环境，同时适应大大小小的变更作为设计流程的日常一部分。你将向项目的战斗主管汇报工作。 职责： 设计、实现并改进战斗机制与系统，使其令玩家满意并提升对游戏概念的信心。 在所处游戏类型中探索创新战斗设计方案，提出多种可行的设计与解决方案，清晰说明各种方案之间的权衡与风险。 利用快速原型与游戏测试验证设计方案的可行性。 编写清晰的设计文档，确保非设计人员也能评估与实现相关内容。 与设计团队及其他跨职能团队协作，共同打造出色的整体游戏体验。积极配合、听取反馈并不断迭代，以实现最高质量的产品。 岗位要求： 至少 3 年专业游戏设计工作经验。 至少 2 年 Unreal 引擎使用经验，熟练使用技能编辑器、关卡编辑器、GAS 系统及蓝图。 在以下领域至少精通一项：战斗机制、战斗手感、战斗 AI、角色控制 / 摄像机 / 操作系统。 对 PvE 动作游戏 / ARPG 类型有深入理解与热情，并积极参与相关游戏体验。 能够流利使用中文普通话交流。 加分项： 在至少一款已发行游戏中，熟练使用某款游戏引擎的脚本编程。 熟悉《英雄联盟》宇宙与 IP。 能流利使用英语进行口头与书面交流。 在此岗位中，你将通过精湛的专业技能、积极的协作精神和以玩家愉悦体验为导向的决策获得成功。我们将重点参考你过往的学习经历、工作经验及你与游戏的个人关系。如果你具有强烈的玩家同理心，并真正关心玩家的游戏体验，这份工作可能正是为你量身打造的！ 用工性质：与专业人事服务公司FESCO Adecco签订劳动合同Apply

apiJob.title: Interactive Music Design ManagerAudioDevelopment StudiosSingapore
apiJob.description:
AudioInteractive Music Design ManagerSingaporeJob Id: REQ-0009720Riot Games was founded on the principles that putting players first leads to exceptional experiences. Since the launch of League of Legends in 2009, millions of players around the world log in each month to connect through our games and the music that brings them to life. As we continue to expand our global content portfolio, we’re seeking an Interactive Music Manager based in Singapore to support the delivery of high-quality interactive music across Riot’s products and partnerships. That's where you come in. As the Interactive Music Design Manager in Riot Singapore Game Studio, you’ll help bring Riot’s music vision to life by developing and overseeing interactive music production and in-game implementation across assigned products and features. This role sits at the intersection of music, audio systems, and gameplay — partnering closely with Audio, Design, Engineering, and external music vendors to ensure music is authored, implemented, and optimized for real-time player experiences. This is not a music supervision, licensing, or marketing-driven role. It is a game music production role with a strong emphasis on music systems, technical integration, and interactive execution, rather than record production or campaign-based music supervision.This is a position based in Singapore, working closely with both global and regional Riot teams. Responsibilities: Production Scope & Accountability Translate gameplay goals, creative vision, and system intent into clear, actionable interactive music briefs. Be responsible for end-to-end interactive music production for game cosmetic products across League of Legends & Teamfight Tactics. Interactive Music Systems & Implementation Own and oversee end-to-end game music delivery across the team, spanning asset creation, in-game implementation, and final validation. Partner with game teams to define and support interactive music system behavior, including transitions, layering, states, and real-time logic. Supervise and review interactive music implementation using middleware or game engines (e.g., Wwise, FMOD, Unreal, Unity or equivalent) and ensure performance considerations are met. Guide and support external composers and vendors on interactive music design best practices for real-time gameplay. Validate music content against runtime performance, memory, streaming, and platform constraints. Ensure profiling, debugging, and iteration of in-game music behavior to ensure a polished player experience. Team Alignment & Collaboration Foster healthy collaboration, craft growth, and accountability across internal team members and external contributors Maintain alignment & relationship building with partners & stakeholders at Riot LAX HQ, including League Creative eXpressions (LSCX) & Riot Games Music (RGM) Facilitate collaboration & communication across global, regional, & external partners Support interactive music team members through goal setting, feedback, and ongoing development. Surface and escalate music-related risks, dependencies, and tradeoffs related to implementation, scope, or technical constraints. Outsource & Resourcing Manage outsource music production & the day-to-day work of external composers and vendors, including interactive design direction, revisions, and delivery expectations Recommend and assess composers and music vendors based on creative fit, interactive design fluency, and technical delivery capability. Set clear expectations for interactive deliverables, implementation readiness, and iteration cycles Creative Execution & Quality Guide & oversee day-to-day creative execution & iteration with external composers, ensuring music supports interactive systems and gameplay needs. Oversee recording, revisions, and final music assets with gameplay constraints in mind. Maintain a high creative and technical quality bar across all music deliverables Provide clear actionable feedback on both musical content and in-game behavior throughout production cycles Constraints & Operations Balance quality, scope, time, and budget within defined production constraints Validate production milestones and deliverables prior to creative sign-off and implementation. Facilitate invoice submission and payment by validating milestones and coordinating with Production and Finance partners Required Qualifications: 5+ years of professional experience composing and technically implementing music for shipped video games Ability to translate creative vision and product intent into clear interactive music design briefs Demonstrated ability to uphold a creative and technical quality bar without serving as a primary composer Individual Contributor Experience managing outsourced music production, including interactive design guidance, revisions, and delivery expectations Sound judgment in assessing composers or music vendors based on interactive design capability and production constraints Working knowledge of interactive music systems and middleware (e.g., Wwise, FMOD or equivalent) Experience collaborating cross-functionally with Audio, Design, Product and Engineering teams Strong understanding of music production workflows, recording processes, and delivery pipelines Ability to balance quality, scope, time, and budget within defined constraints Experience validating production milestones and facilitating invoices with Production and Finance Strong communication and stakeholder collaboration skills across disciplines and regions Desired Qualifications: Previous experience working at a games developer or publisher Hands-on familiarity with Wwise, Unreal, Unity, or proprietary game engines. Deep fandom or working knowledge of Riot IPs & their cultural/music identities. Experience managing outsource vendors, composers and musicians. Experience working with international teams and global music talent. Our Perks: Full relocation support Full health insurance for you, your spouse, and children Open paid time off Retirement benefits with company matching Life insurance, parental leave, plus short-term and long-term disability Play Fund so you can broaden and deepen your knowledge of our players and community through games We will double down on your donations of time and money to non-profits Don’t forget to include a resume and cover letter. We receive a lot of applications, but we’ll notice a fun, well-written intro that shows us you take play seriously. Apply

apiJob.title: Level Design Lead - VALORANT
apiJob.description:
DesignLevel Design Lead - VALORANTLos Angeles, USAAdditional Locations: Mercer Island, USAJob Id: REQ-0009665As a Game Design Manager / Level Design Lead you'll be front and center in defining the design of our game. You'll partner with leads from multiple disciplines to ensure that VALORANT’s tactical gameplay shines through. You'll be a champion for design excellence, and level up those around you. You’ll apply rigorous critical thinking and a strong dose of imagination to engineer the fun of thrilling in-game experiences. You'll work with every tool at hand and collaborate with multiple interdisciplinary teams to create player-focused designs that'll delight millions around the world. VALORANT is Riot’s take on a character-driven tactical shooter—one where every angle matters, every decision carries weight, and competitive integrity is sacred. As Level Design Lead, you’ll help define the future of VALORANT’s maps: evolving existing spaces, leading bold new innovations, and ensuring every map delivers on the tactical depth players expect. You’ll partner closely with design, art, and production leadership to shape how millions of players experience the game—today and years from now. This role is both strategic and hands-on. You’ll lead a small, talented team of level designers while remaining deeply connected to the craft—jumping into the editor, guiding design decisions, and helping ship meaningful updates and reworks to a live, competitive game. You’ll report to the Director, Game Design and work at the heart of VALORANT’s gameplay evolution. Responsibilities: Set and evolve the design vision for VALORANT maps, ensuring they uphold the game’s tactical DNA while pushing the genre forward Lead both live map updates and major reworks, building on successes like Breeze and shaping how players engage with maps over time Drive innovation on new maps, exploring new ideas while protecting competitive integrity Manage, mentor, and grow a team of level designers, leveling up their craft through hands-on feedback, coaching, and example Partner closely with Map Art, Lighting, and Production, blending gameplay clarity with visual quality and shipping reliably at high polish Guide projects from early concept through final release, balancing player value, development cost, and long-term sustainability Influence senior leadership on product direction, strategy, and priorities related to maps and gameplay experiences Anticipate player needs across platforms, understanding how different audiences engage with competitive shooters Champion player empathy, using data, feedback, and intuition to evolve maps in ways that resonate with the community Required Qualifications: 5+ years experience in a Level Design role; experience as a game design owner of multiple large projects in parallel with successful delivery 1+ years people management experience; experience with mentorship of other designers leading to a promotion or substantial positive improvements in their work Experience creating systemic change to a design team’s process or principles to improve output significantly across the team Experience leading cross-discipline teams to deliver on design goals Experience communicating and expressing complex and nuanced designs in simple ways Leadership experience resolving conflict, and when needed offering difficult feedback with respect Desired Qualifications: Experience working as a developer on an FPS title. Strong understanding of Tactical FPS principles and can apply them effectively Robust understanding of the FPS market and player motivations and behaviors Can quickly produce a wide breadth of mechanics solutions, and generate and identify an excellent solution Experience developing on multiple platforms For this role, you'll find success through craft expertise and a collaborative spirit that prioritizes the delight of players. We will look at your past studies and experience, but for this role, we also look for dedicated people with a personal relationship with games. If you embody player empathy and care about players' experiences, this is the role for you! Our Perks: Riot focuses on work/life balance, shown by our open paid time off policy and other perks such as flexible work schedules. We offer medical, dental, and life insurance, parental leave for you, your spouse/domestic partner, and children, and a 401k with company match. Check out our benefits pages for more information. At Riot Games, we put players first. That mission drives every decision in our quest to create games and experiences that make it better to be a player. Whether you’re working directly on a new player-facing experience or you’re supporting the company as a whole, everyone at Riot is part of our mission. And just like in our games, we’re better when we work together. Our goal is to create collaborative teams where you are empowered to bring your unique perspective everyday. If that sounds like the kind of place you want to work, we’re looking forward to your application. It’s our policy to provide equal employment opportunity for all applicants and members of Riot Games, Inc. Riot Games makes reasonable accommodations for handicapped and disabled Rioters and does not unlawfully discriminate on the basis of race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, national origin, age, handicap, veteran status, marital status, criminal history, or any other category protected by applicable federal and state law. We consider for employment all qualified applicants, including those with criminal histories, in a manner consistent with applicable federal, state and local law, including the California Fair Chance Act, the City of Los Angeles Fair Chance Initiative for Hiring Ordinance, the Los Angeles County Fair Chance Ordinance for Employers, the San Francisco Fair Chance Ordinance, and the Washington Fair Chance Act. Per the Los Angeles County Fair Chance Ordinance, the following core duties may create a basis for disqualifying candidates with relevant criminal histories: Safeguarding confidential and sensitive Company data Communication with others, including Rioters and third parties such as vendors, and/or players, including minors Accessing Company assets, secure digital systems, and networks Ensuring a safe interactive environment for players and other Rioters These duties are directly related to essential operations, safety, trust, and compliance obligations within our organization. Please note that job duties may evolve based on business needs and additional responsibilities may be assigned as necessary to maintain operational efficiency and security. (Los Angeles Only) Base salary range between $166,600.00 - $232,800.00 USD + incentive compensation + equity + 401K with company match + medical, dental, vision, and life insurance + short and long-term disability + open PTO.(Mercer Island only) Base salary range between $166,600.00 - $232,800.00 USD + incentive compensation + equity + 401K with company match + medical, dental, vision, and life insurance + short and long-term disability + open PTO.Apply

apiJob.title: Manager, 3D Character Art - Teamfight Tactics
apiJob.description:
ArtManager, 3D Character Art - Teamfight TacticsSingaporeJob Id: REQ-0009606Riot Games was established in 2006 by entrepreneurial gamers who believe that player-focused game development can result in great games. In 2009, Riot released its debut title League of Legends to critical and player acclaim. As the most played PC game in the world, over 100 million play every month. Players form the foundation of our community and it’s for them that we continue to evolve and improve the League of Legends experience. We’re looking for humble but ambitious, razor-sharp professionals who can teach us a thing or two. We promise to return the favor. Like us, you take play seriously; you’re passionate about games. We embrace those who see things differently, aren’t afraid to experiment, and who have a healthy disregard for constraints. That's where you come in. As an 3D Character Lead in the Singapore Studio Teamfight Tactics Cosmetic team, you will channel your knowledge of color and light to explore and produce the best player value for Teamfight Tactics players. You’ll guide the exploration and execution of the 3D discipline across Character and Environment content. In addition to providing clear direction, you’ll implement the sleek, dynamic and dramatic visual effects that add atmosphere and depth to the gameplay. Through your expertise, you’ll mentor and level-up the craft and game developer skills of your 3D team. You will work with the Associate Art Director to implement a 3D style that fits within the art pillars and punctuates the game design. You will implement the effects and set the high quality bar. You’ll expand and support a high-performing external team and give them guidance and knowledge. You will set high expectations, push for growth for both each individual artist and the entire team, and streamline the production efficiency. You will use your broad knowledge of art, craft, technique, and people management, to set clear goals for your team and make sure the process of delivering those goals is as smooth as possible for your team and their stakeholders. You will ensure your team stays motivated and empowered to take ownership of creativity, you will coach your team to be a triple A team. You will work closely with Product Leads, Delivery Leads, Stakeholders, and the Associate Art Director to stay aligned on vision and priorities. Quickly mediate and resolve craft disagreements and misalignments. Prioritize the craft’s health and support, above personally contributing to art production. Run and manage healthy and productive craft review sessions. Work closely with the others 3D Leads on Teamfight Tactics Cosmetic to together cultivate a great cohseive culture and ensure a consistent quality and style. Responsibilities: As a 3D lead on the team, you will oversee and be accountable for the quality of the work the team delivers and set the bar for excellence. Provide craft direction for your team. Provide as-needed support of all aspects of 3D production process; from set-up through production and troubleshooting You will balance visual satisfaction and gameplay clarity. Provide hands on craft support to the product where needed Support the Associate Art Director on emergent tasks where needed. You will build a world-class team by sourcing AAA 3D artists in China and developing and growing their craft skills and soft skills through training and feedback Build and maintain a healthy 3D team. Set clear vision, provide consistent feedback, and build trust. You will establish and maintain a highly engaged, creative, and motivated culture to unleash artists’ potential. You will drive and improve the rituals in your team to support high-quality craft delivery and enable efficient growth of your team. You will collaborate with Rioters in Singapore and worldwide to make sure product goals are fulfilled, and working practices are appropriate. You will be vigilantly finding creative and effective solutions to eliminate complex problems. Required Qualifications: 5+ years professional experience in the video games industry in 3D. 2+ years of management experience of leading/mentoring teams and individuals. Advanced skills in 3D/2D Software - Maya, 3DS Max, Photoshop Experience creating high-quality, stylized 3D models and textures. Expertise in handpainted stylized. Shipped titles as a Senior or Lead 3D Artist High proficiency in 3D production development, including fundamentals such as timing, shape language, composition, design, shader and color theory A clear understanding of the Teamfight Tactics art style Desired Qualifications: AAA Feature/Game experience Teamfight Tactics community connections/knowledge Experience giving direction and delivering clear, constructive feedback to vendors Past leadership or management experience Development experience creating materials and PBR assets within modern game engines (Unreal, Unity, etc) Broad experience of collaborating with other art disciplines, working production end to end Well-versed in 3D work design for themed and cosmetic content across a variety of game titles, with the ability to form and articulate thoughtful creative insights. Tools experience: have worked in several tools, pipelines, and game engines Fluent in Mandarin Chinese and English, with the ability to communicate effectively in multilingual, cross-cultural treatments Experience managing distributed teams across time zones and in remote work environments Our Perks Full relocation support Full health insurance for you, your spouse, and children Open paid time off Retirement benefits with company matching Life insurance, parental leave, plus short-term and long-term disability Play Fund so you can broaden and deepen your knowledge of our players and community through games We will double down on your donations of time and money to non-profits Apply
